name: Rwanda

visa_free.name: Federation of Saint Christopher and Nevis

outbound_official.notes: Source: Rwanda Directorate General of Immigration and Emigration (migration.gov.rw), official 'List of countries that Rwandan passport holders travel visa free' page. The page groups destinations into 'Visa Not Required', 'Visa Upon Arrival', and 'Visa Free on Arrival' (South Sudan). South Sudan is placed under visa_on_arrival here as the page labels it 'Visa Free on Arrival' (a distinct sub-category from fully visa-not-required). No date stamp was present on the source page at time of retrieval (2026-06-14). No e-visa or ETA categories are listed on this official outbound page.

visa_free.nationality: East African Community (EAC) member states citizens (Burundi, Kenya, Uganda, United Republic of Tanzania, South Sudan)
visa_free.notes: Citizens of EAC Partner States are issued a Visitor's Pass (VP-1) free of charge upon arrival, valid for up to six months. Citizens of Uganda and Kenya may enter using their national ID card. No permit fees apply for EAC citizens. This is a genuine entry pass — not a visa on arrival — consistent with EAC free-movement obligations.

visa_on_arrival.nationality: African Union, Commonwealth and La Francophonie member states
visa_on_arrival.notes: Citizens of countries that are members of the African Union, Commonwealth, or La Francophonie receive a visa on arrival with the visa fee waived (free of charge) for a stay of 30 days. The visa is still formally issued upon arrival — it is not a visa-free pass. Countries with a full bilateral visa waiver arrangement with Rwanda receive 90 days free. See the official Rwanda visa regime communique (March 2020 PDF) for the enumerated list of eligible states.
Free visa on arrival for 90 days per official visa-on-arrival page.

visa_on_arrival.nationality: All other nationalities (not visa-exempt or free-visa eligible)
visa_on_arrival.notes: From 1 January 2018, nationals of all countries can obtain a visa on arrival at Kigali International Airport and all land borders without prior application. Fee: USD 50 for single entry (30 days). Multiple entry: USD 70 (90 days). Applicants also have the option to apply online via irembo.gov.rw before departure.

e_visa.notes: Online visa applications accepted via irembo.gov.rw for all nationalities. USD 50 for single entry (30 days); USD 70 for multiple entry (90 days). Fee is waived for nationals of countries with a visa waiver arrangement (AU, Commonwealth, La Francophonie, or bilateral 90-day waiver). Applicants may pay online or upon arrival.

visa_policy.notes: Rwanda operates a highly open visa policy since 1 January 2018. EAC citizens get a genuine visitor's pass (visa-free) for up to 6 months. African Union, Commonwealth and La Francophonie member nationals receive a free visa on arrival for 30 days (fee waived, but a visa is still formally issued). An explicit list of 19 countries (Angola, Benin, CAR, Chad, Cote d'Ivoire, DRC, Saint Kitts & Nevis, Ghana, Guinea, Indonesia, Haiti, Mauritius, Philippines, Senegal, Seychelles, Sierra Leone, Sao Tome & Principe, Singapore, Qatar) receive a free 90-day visa on arrival. All other nationalities pay USD 50 (single entry, 30 days) or USD 70 (multiple entry, 90 days) on arrival or via e-visa. The East Africa Tourist Visa (EATV/V11) at USD 100 covers Rwanda, Kenya, and Uganda with multiple entries valid for 90 days. CLASSIFICATION NOTE: AU/Commonwealth/La Francophonie and 19-country free entries are recorded as visa_on_arrival (free) rather than visa_free, consistent with Rwanda's own terminology ('visa on arrival with fee waiver') on migration.gov.rw. Only the EAC pass is truly visa_free.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic and service/official passports of the listed 29 countries are granted visa-free entry for 90 days under bilateral visa suppression agreements. China also includes Public Affairs passport holders. These exemptions apply only to diplomatic/service/official passport holders; ordinary passport holders of these countries are subject to standard visa-on-arrival or free-visa-on-arrival rules by nationality.

Rwanda and Russia signed a visa waiver agreement for holders of diplomatic and service passports. The agreement was first signed in Sochi on 9 November 2024 and a further instrument was signed in Antalya on 18 April 2026. Entry into force date is not confirmed on any official Rwanda government source as of June 2026. Included here as a signed agreement; may not yet be operational. Stay duration not published.

Rwanda and Venezuela signed a Memorandum of Understanding on visa waiver for holders of Diplomatic, Official, and Special passports, signed in Kigali on 1 November 2023. Entry into force date is not confirmed on any official Rwanda government source. Stay duration not published.

passport_types: unknown — not specified in official source
conditional_access.conditions: Rwanda and Maldives signed an Agreement on Mutual Visa Exemption on 5 May 2023. Passport types covered (ordinary, diplomatic, official) and stay duration are not specified in the official announcement on minaffet.gov.rw. Entry into force date not confirmed. Included as a signed agreement pending detail confirmation.

cbi.notes: Rwanda does not have a formal standalone Citizenship-by-Investment (CBI) program with defined investment thresholds. The official citizenship page lists 'Substantial sustainable investments or activities' as one of several discretionary grounds for citizenship by acquisition (naturalization), but no minimum investment amount, program name, or dedicated CBI processing pathway is published on the official migration site. Applicants on investment grounds must provide proof of registration as an investor and a certificate of sustainable occupation/activities. Application fees range from 5,000 to 150,000 RWF depending on pathway. Processing time for citizenship applications is 6 months. No advertised CBI product found on any official Rwanda government domain.

programs.name: Business Investor Resident Permits (Temporary) – multiple sector categories
programs.notes: Multiple investor permit categories for foreign investors certified by Rwanda Development Board (RDB), covering: mining (A1/A3-1), agriculture/animal husbandry (B1/B3-1), manufacturing (F1/F3-1), hospitality (O1/O3-1), IT (W1/W3-1), transport/logistics (X1/X3-1), other sectors (Z1/Z3-1). New permit fee: 150,000 RWF (up to 2 years). Recurring permit fee: 250,000 RWF (up to 5 years). No minimum investment threshold published on official pages. Citizenship by residence requires 15 consecutive years.

programs.name: Person with Assured Income Permit (K Permit)
programs.notes: Issued to persons with assured income including retired persons who do not intend to be employed in Rwanda. Minimum age: 55 years. Minimum income: USD 5,000 per month required.

programs.name: Itinerant Business Visa (V9) – Long-term Investor Visa
programs.notes: Issued to a foreign investor, board member or representative of a multinational company who does not reside in Rwanda but has significant business activities in the country and needs to regularly visit. Fee: USD 200. Valid for 2 to 10 years (renewable). Multiple entries. This is a long-stay visa category rather than a full residence permit.

programs.name: Entrepreneurship Visa (2-year) – Startup Founders
programs.eligibility: Foreign start-up founders and innovative entrepreneurs and their dependents
programs.notes: 2-year entrepreneurship visa allowing foreign startup founders to start a business in Rwanda. Based on Investment Promotion and Facilitation Law No. 006/2021 of 8 February 2021. Detailed in RRA Investment Incentives document (August 2023) Section XVII (Talent Attraction Incentives). No dedicated application portal found on migration.gov.rw or irembo.gov.rw.

programs.name: Talent Visa (2-year) – International Graduates
programs.eligibility: Qualifying international students from qualifying higher institutions of learning
programs.notes: 2-year talent visa commencing from the date of completion of studies for qualifying international graduates. Detailed in RRA Investment Incentives document (August 2023) Section XVII. No dedicated application portal found on migration.gov.rw or irembo.gov.rw.

programs.name: Remote Worker Visa (2-year)
programs.eligibility: Qualifying remote workers in priority professional fields
programs.notes: 2-year visa allowing qualifying remote workers to live in Rwanda and legally work for an employer registered abroad or their own company. Detailed in RRA Investment Incentives document (August 2023) Section XVII. No dedicated application portal found on migration.gov.rw or irembo.gov.rw at time of research.

programs.name: Skilled Worker Permit – Occupations on Demand (H1)
programs.eligibility: Skilled workers whose occupation appears on the official Occupations on Demand List (ODL) published by the Directorate General of Immigration
programs.notes: Temporary resident permit (H1) for skilled workers on the Occupations on Demand List. No labour market test required (unlike the H2 sponsored category). A job-search visa (V4) of 90 days at USD 50 is also available for workers on the ODL to seek employment before applying for an H1 permit.

programs.name: East Africa Tourist Visa (EATV / V11)
programs.eligibility: Foreign tourists intending to visit Rwanda, Kenya, and Uganda
programs.notes: USD 100; valid 90 days with multiple entries covering Rwanda, Kenya and Uganda. Available on arrival, online via irembo.gov.rw, or at Rwandan diplomatic missions abroad. Formally classified as visa type V11 on the official visitors-visa page.

agreements: East African Community (EAC) – free movement/entry pass for EAC citizens (up to 6 months)
African Union (AU) – free visa on arrival (fee waived) for AU member citizens (30 days)
Commonwealth – free visa on arrival (fee waived) for Commonwealth citizens (30 days)
La Francophonie (OIF) – free visa on arrival (fee waived) for La Francophonie member citizens (30 days)
CEPGL (Economic Community of the Great Lakes Countries) – regional trade permits (G1) for CEPGL/EAC nationals
Bilateral diplomatic/service passport visa suppression agreements with 29 countries (see conditional_access)
Rwanda-Russia – diplomatic and service passport visa waiver (signed Nov 2024 and Apr 2026; entry into force unconfirmed)
Rwanda-Venezuela – diplomatic, official and special passport visa waiver MOU (signed Nov 2023; entry into force unconfirmed)
Rwanda-Maldives – mutual visa exemption (signed May 2023; passport types and entry into force unconfirmed)

validation_notes: CORRECTED (classification): AU/Commonwealth/La Francophonie free entry and the 19-country 90-day free entry were previously in visa_policy.visa_free but are formally issued as visa-on-arrival by Rwanda (migration.gov.rw calls them 'visa on arrival with fee waiver'). Moved to visa_policy.visa_on_arrival. Only the EAC visitor's pass remains as true visa_free.

ADDED (conditional_access): Rwanda-Russia diplomatic/service passport visa waiver agreement (signed Sochi 9 Nov 2024; second signing Antalya 18 Apr 2026) added to conditional_access. Entry into force unconfirmed on official sources.
ADDED (conditional_access): Rwanda-Venezuela MOU for diplomatic/official/special passport holders (signed 1 Nov 2023) added to conditional_access. Entry into force unconfirmed.
ADDED (conditional_access): Rwanda-Maldives mutual visa exemption (signed 5 May 2023) added to conditional_access. Passport types and stay duration not specified in official announcement.
VERIFIED: 29-country diplomatic/service/official passport list (conditional_access entry 1) confirmed unchanged from rwandainkenya.gov.rw.
VERIFIED: 19-country list of 90-day free-visa-on-arrival nations confirmed unchanged from migration.gov.rw/visa-on-arrival.
VERIFIED: EAC 6-month visitor's pass confirmed unchanged.
VERIFIED: CBI — no formal program confirmed; no minimum investment threshold published.
VERIFIED: RBI investor permit fees confirmed (150,000 RWF new, 250,000 RWF recurring); no minimum investment threshold.
UPDATED: EATV now noted as visa type V11 per visitors-visa page classification.
UPDATED: CBI notes expanded to include fee range (5,000–150,000 RWF) sourced from citizenship page.

ADDED (outbound_official): Rwanda Directorate General of Immigration official outbound page (migration.gov.rw/1-1/sops-1) located and recorded. Lists 48 visa-free destinations and 18 visa-on-arrival destinations for Rwanda ordinary passport holders.
gaps: The March 2020 official visa regime communique PDF was partially cut off; the complete enumerated list of AU/Commonwealth/La Francophonie countries granted 30-day free visa on arrival is not fully legible from the official PDF.
No specific minimum investment amount for any investor residence permit is stated on official migration.gov.rw pages; amounts are absent, not merely omitted by this researcher.
Permanent Permits page (migration.gov.rw/our-services/permit/permanent-permits) only states 'The applicant should contact DGIE before making any application' — no published criteria or thresholds.

The 2-year Remote Worker Visa, Talent Visa, and Entrepreneurship Visa are referenced in the RRA Investment Incentives PDF (August 2023) under Investment Law No. 006/2021, but no dedicated application portal or detailed requirements page was found on irembo.gov.rw or migration.gov.rw.

Dual citizenship rules for Rwanda not explicitly stated on official immigration pages; renunciation of Rwandan nationality is addressed but active dual-citizenship holding status is unclear from official sources.
No formal CBI program with fixed investment thresholds found on any official Rwanda government domain.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS — No official Rwanda government source found granting entry based on holding a third-country credential (US visa, Schengen visa, UK visa, residence permit, etc.). Not recorded per hard classification rules.
CONDITIONAL ACCESS — No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) or similar diaspora/heritage special-status entry rules found for Rwanda on any official source.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS — Rwanda-Russia visa waiver: signed twice (Nov 2024 Sochi; Apr 2026 Antalya) but no entry-into-force date published on any official Rwandan government source. Operational status uncertain.
CONDITIONAL ACCESS — Rwanda-Venezuela MOU (signed Nov 2023): entry into force unconfirmed on official sources.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS — Rwanda-Maldives mutual visa exemption (signed May 2023): passport types covered (ordinary vs diplomatic only) and stay duration not specified in official minaffet.gov.rw announcement.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS — Rwanda-Azerbaijan and Rwanda-Kazakhstan diplomatic-service passport agreements (noted on aggregator sources as signed Sep 2023 and Sep 2024 respectively) not found confirmed on any official Rwandan government source; not recorded.

VP-2 Visitor's Pass for Regional Blocs: the official visitors-visa page references a 'VP-2' category for citizens of regional blocs at community-determined fees and up to 6 months, but the specific blocs and conditions are not enumerated on the page; not recorded without a complete official list.
OUTBOUND — outbound_official page (migration.gov.rw/1-1/sops-1) carries no publication date; currency of the list cannot be confirmed with a timestamp.
